Formulas

Complete documentation for Excel formulas in Aspose.Cells FOSS for .NET

← Back to Index

Formula Overview

Aspose.Cells FOSS provides comprehensive support for Excel formulas, allowing you to create, read, and calculate formulas just like in Microsoft Excel. Formulas enable powerful calculations, data analysis, and dynamic spreadsheet functionality.

Key Formula Features:
  • Create formulas using standard Excel syntax
  • Read existing formulas from workbooks
  • Support for arithmetic, logical, text, date, and lookup functions
  • Cell references (absolute and relative)
  • Range references and operations
  • Named ranges and defined names
  • Formula calculation and recalculation

Setting Formulas

Basic Formula Assignment
var cell = worksheet.Cells["A5"];

// Set a simple formula (without '=' prefix)
cell.Formula = "SUM(A1:A4)";

// Or with '=' prefix
cell.Formula = "=SUM(A1:A4)";

// Both are valid
Formula Examples
// Arithmetic operations
cells["A1"].Formula = "=B1 + C1";
cells["A2"].Formula = "=B2 - C2";
cells["A3"].Formula = "=B3 * C3";
cells["A4"].Formula = "=B4 / C4";

// Percentage calculation
cells["A5"].Formula = "=B5 * 0.10";

// Simple average
cells["A6"].Formula = "=AVERAGE(A1:A4)";

// Maximum value
cells["A7"].Formula = "=MAX(A1:A4)";

// Minimum value
cells["A8"].Formula = "=MIN(A1:A4)";

// Count cells
cells["A9"].Formula = "=COUNT(A1:A4)";

// Conditional sum
cells["A10"].Formula = "=SUMIF(A1:A4, \">100\")";
Cell References
// Relative references (change when copied)
cells["A1"].Formula = "=B1 + C1";

// Absolute references (do not change when copied)
cells["A2"].Formula = "=$B$1 + $C$1";

// Mixed references
cells["A3"].Formula = "=$B1 + C$1";  // Column B is absolute, row 1 is relative

// Cross-worksheet references
cells["A4"].Formula = "=Sheet2!A1";
cells["A5"].Formula = "='Sheet 2'!A1";  // Spaces in sheet name require quotes

// 3D references (across multiple worksheets)
cells["A6"].Formula = "=SUM(Sheet1:Sheet3!A1)";

Getting Formulas

Reading Formula Strings
var cell = worksheet.Cells["A5"];

// Check if cell contains a formula
if (cell.ContainsFormula)
{
    // Get the formula string
    string formula = cell.Formula;
    Console.WriteLine($"Formula: {formula}");
}
else
{
    Console.WriteLine("Cell does not contain a formula");
}

// Check formula type
if (cell.Type == CellValueType.IsFormula)
{
    Console.WriteLine("Cell contains a formula");
}
Formula vs Value
var cell = worksheet.Cells["A5"];

// Get the formula string
string formula = cell.Formula;  // e.g., "=SUM(A1:A4)"

// Get the calculated value
object value = cell.Value;  // e.g., 42.5

// Get the display string
string displayString = cell.DisplayStringValue;  // e.g., "42.5"

// Get the string representation
string stringValue = cell.StringValue;  // Same as Value.ToString()

Console.WriteLine($"Formula: {formula}");
Console.WriteLine($"Value: {value}");
Console.WriteLine($"Display: {displayString}");

Common Formula Categories

Arithmetic Functions
// Sum
cells["A1"].Formula = "=SUM(A2:A10)";
cells["A2"].Formula = "=SUM(A2, A4, A6)";

// Average
cells["A3"].Formula = "=AVERAGE(A2:A10)";

// Count
cells["A4"].Formula = "=COUNT(A2:A10)";
cells["A5"].Formula = "=COUNTA(A2:A10)";  // Count non-empty cells

// Product
cells["A6"].Formula = "=PRODUCT(A2:A5)";

// Power
cells["A7"].Formula = "=POWER(A2, 2)";
cells["A8"].Formula = "=A2^2";

// Square root
cells["A9"].Formula = "=SQRT(A2)";

// Absolute value
cells["A10"].Formula = "=ABS(A2)";

// Round
cells["A11"].Formula = "=ROUND(A2, 2)";  // Round to 2 decimal places
cells["A12"].Formula = "=ROUNDUP(A2, 0)";
cells["A13"].Formula = "=ROUNDDOWN(A2, 0)";
Statistical Functions
// Maximum and minimum
cells["A1"].Formula = "=MAX(A2:A10)";
cells["A2"].Formula = "=MIN(A2:A10)";

// Median
cells["A3"].Formula = "=MEDIAN(A2:A10)";

// Mode
cells["A4"].Formula = "=MODE(A2:A10)";

// Standard deviation
cells["A5"].Formula = "=STDEV(A2:A10)";
cells["A6"].Formula = "=STDEVP(A2:A10)";

// Variance
cells["A7"].Formula = "=VAR(A2:A10)";
cells["A8"].Formula = "=VARP(A2:A10)";

// Rank
cells["A9"].Formula = "=RANK(A2, A2:A10)";

// Percentile
cells["A10"].Formula = "=PERCENTILE(A2:A10, 0.5)";  // 50th percentile
Logical Functions
// IF statement
cells["A1"].Formula = "=IF(A2 > 100, \"High\", \"Low\")";

// Nested IF
cells["A2"].Formula = "=IF(A3 > 100, \"Very High\", IF(A3 > 50, \"Medium\", \"Low\"))";

// AND
cells["A3"].Formula = "=AND(A2 > 10, A2 < 100)";

// OR
cells["A4"].Formula = "=OR(A2 = 10, A2 = 20, A2 = 30)";

// NOT
cells["A5"].Formula = "=NOT(A2 > 100)";

// IFERROR
cells["A6"].Formula = "=IFERROR(A2/B2, \"Division by zero\")";

// TRUE and FALSE
cells["A7"].Formula = "=TRUE()";
cells["A8"].Formula = "=FALSE()";
Text Functions
// Concatenate
cells["A1"].Formula = "=CONCATENATE(A2, \" \", B2)";
cells["A2"].Formula = "=A2 & \" \" & B2";

// Length
cells["A3"].Formula = "=LEN(A2)";

// Upper and lower case
cells["A4"].Formula = "=UPPER(A2)";
cells["A5"].Formula = "=LOWER(A2)";
cells["A6"].Formula = "=PROPER(A2)";

// Extract characters
cells["A7"].Formula = "=LEFT(A2, 5)";
cells["A8"].Formula = "=RIGHT(A2, 5)";
cells["A9"].Formula = "=MID(A2, 3, 5)";

// Find and search
cells["A10"].Formula = "=FIND(\"text\", A2)";
cells["A11"].Formula = "=SEARCH(\"text\", A2)";

// Replace
cells["A12"].Formula = "=REPLACE(A2, 1, 5, \"new\")";

// Trim
cells["A13"].Formula = "=TRIM(A2)";

// Value conversion
cells["A14"].Formula = "=VALUE(A2)";
cells["A15"].Formula = "=TEXT(A2, \"yyyy-mm-dd\")";
Date and Time Functions
// Current date and time
cells["A1"].Formula = "=TODAY()";
cells["A2"].Formula = "=NOW()";

// Date components
cells["A3"].Formula = "=YEAR(A1)";
cells["A4"].Formula = "=MONTH(A1)";
cells["A5"].Formula = "=DAY(A1)";

// Time components
cells["A6"].Formula = "=HOUR(A2)";
cells["A7"].Formula = "=MINUTE(A2)";
cells["A8"].Formula = "=SECOND(A2)";

// Date arithmetic
cells["A9"].Formula = "=A1 + 7";  // Add 7 days
cells["A10"].Formula = "=A1 - A3";  // Days between dates

// Create date
cells["A11"].Formula = "=DATE(2026, 5, 13)";
cells["A12"].Formula = "=DATEVALUE(\"2026-05-13\")";

// Workday calculations
cells["A13"].Formula = "=WORKDAY(A1, 10)";
cells["A14"].Formula = "=NETWORKDAYS(A1, A10)";

// Weekday
cells["A15"].Formula = "=WEEKDAY(A1)";
cells["A16"].Formula = "=TEXT(A1, \"dddd\")";  // Day of week name
Lookup and Reference Functions
// VLOOKUP
cells["A1"].Formula = "=VLOOKUP(A2, Sheet2!A:C, 3, FALSE)";

// HLOOKUP
cells["A2"].Formula = "=HLOOKUP(A3, Sheet2!A1:D10, 3, FALSE)";

// INDEX and MATCH
cells["A3"].Formula = "=INDEX(A2:C10, MATCH(A4, A2:A10, 0), 3)";

// OFFSET
cells["A4"].Formula = "=OFFSET(A1, 2, 1, 5, 3)";

// ADDRESS
cells["A5"].Formula = "=ADDRESS(2, 3)";

// INDIRECT
cells["A6"].Formula = "=INDIRECT(\"A\" & A1)";

// ROW and COLUMN
cells["A7"].Formula = "=ROW()";
cells["A8"].Formula = "=COLUMN()";
Conditional Functions
// SUMIF
cells["A1"].Formula = "=SUMIF(A2:A10, \">100\")";
cells["A2"].Formula = "=SUMIF(A2:A10, \">100\", B2:B10)";

// COUNTIF
cells["A3"].Formula = "=COUNTIF(A2:A10, \">100\")";
cells["A4"].Formula = "=COUNTIF(A2:A10, \"apples\")";

// AVERAGEIF
cells["A5"].Formula = "=AVERAGEIF(A2:A10, \">100\")";

// SUMIFS (multiple conditions)
cells["A6"].Formula = "=SUMIFS(B2:B10, A2:A10, \">100\", C2:C10, \"east\")";

// COUNTIFS
cells["A7"].Formula = "=COUNTIFS(A2:A10, \">100\", B2:B10, \"east\")";

// AVERAGEIFS
cells["A8"].Formula = "=AVERAGEIFS(B2:B10, A2:A10, \">100\", C2:C10, \"east\")";

Formula Calculation

Manual Calculation
// When you set a formula, the value is automatically calculated
cells["A5"].Formula = "=SUM(A1:A4)";

// The calculated value is immediately available
double sum = Convert.ToDouble(cells["A5"].Value);
Console.WriteLine($"Sum: {sum}");

// You can also trigger recalculation
worksheet.CalculateFormula();

// Calculate all formulas in workbook
workbook.CalculateFormula();
Calculation Order
// Formulas are calculated in dependency order
cells["A1"].PutValue(10);
cells["A2"].PutValue(20);
cells["A3"].Formula = "=A1 + A2";  // Result: 30

cells["A4"].Formula = "=A3 * 2";   // Result: 60

// Changing A1 updates all dependent formulas
cells["A1"].PutValue(15);
// A3 becomes 35, A4 becomes 70
Circular References
// Circular references are detected and handled
cells["A1"].Formula = "=A2 + 10";
cells["A2"].Formula = "=A1 + 5";

// This creates a circular reference
// Check workbook settings for circular reference handling
if (workbook.Settings.IterativeCalculation)
{
    Console.WriteLine("Iterative calculation enabled");
}

Named Ranges and Defined Names

Using Named Ranges
// Add a named range
workbook.DefinedNames.Add("SalesData", "Sheet1!$A$1:$D$10");

// Use named range in formulas
cells["E1"].Formula = "=SUM(SalesData)";
cells["E2"].Formula = "=AVERAGE(SalesData)";

// Access named range by name
var salesData = workbook.DefinedNames["SalesData"];
Console.WriteLine($"Refers to: {salesData.RefersTo}");
Dynamic Named Ranges
// Create a dynamic named range using OFFSET
workbook.DefinedNames.Add("DynamicData", "=OFFSET(Sheet1!$A$1, 0, 0, COUNTA(Sheet1!$A:$A), 3)");

// Use in formula
cells["E3"].Formula = "=SUM(DynamicData)";
// Automatically adjusts as data is added

Complete Example

Create Financial Calculator
using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;
using System;

class Program
{
    static void Main()
    {
        var workbook = new Workbook();
        var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];
        var cells = worksheet.Cells;

        // Create header
        cells["A1"].PutValue("Item");
        cells["B1"].PutValue("Quantity");
        cells["C1"].PutValue("Unit Price");
        cells["D1"].PutValue("Total");

        // Apply header styling
        var headerStyle = cells["A1"].GetStyle();
        headerStyle.Font.Bold = true;
        headerStyle.BackgroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.FromArgb(0, 51, 153);
        headerStyle.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;
        for (int col = 0; col <= 3; col++)
        {
            cells[0, col].SetStyle(headerStyle);
        }

        // Add sample data
        string[] items = { "Widget A", "Widget B", "Widget C", "Widget D", "Widget E" };
        int[] quantities = { 10, 15, 20, 8, 12 };
        double[] prices = { 25.50, 30.75, 18.99, 42.00, 15.50 };

        for (int i = 0; i < items.Length; i++)
        {
            int row = i + 2;
            cells[row, 0].PutValue(items[i]);
            cells[row, 1].PutValue(quantities[i]);
            cells[row, 2].PutValue(prices[i]);

            // Add formula for total
            cells[row, 3].Formula = $"=B{row}*C{row}";
        }

        // Add summary calculations
        int summaryRow = items.Length + 3;

        cells[summaryRow, 0].PutValue("Subtotal:");
        cells[summaryRow, 3].Formula = $"=SUM(D2:D{summaryRow - 1})";

        summaryRow++;
        cells[summaryRow, 0].PutValue("Tax (10%):");
        cells[summaryRow, 3].Formula = $"=D{summaryRow - 1} * 0.10";

        summaryRow++;
        cells[summaryRow, 0].PutValue("Total:");
        cells[summaryRow, 3].Formula = $"=D{summaryRow - 2} + D{summaryRow - 1}";

        summaryRow++;
        cells[summaryRow, 0].PutValue("Average Total:");
        cells[summaryRow, 3].Formula = $"=AVERAGE(D2:D{items.Length + 2})";

        summaryRow++;
        cells[summaryRow, 0].PutValue("Max Total:");
        cells[summaryRow, 3].Formula = $"=MAX(D2:D{items.Length + 2})";

        summaryRow++;
        cells[summaryRow, 0].PutValue("Min Total:");
        cells[summaryRow, 3].Formula = $"=MIN(D2:D{items.Length + 2})";

        // Apply currency formatting
        var currencyStyle = workbook.CreateStyle();
        currencyStyle.NumberFormat = "$#,##0.00";

        for (int row = 2; row <= items.Length + 2; row++)
        {
            cells[row, 2].SetStyle(currencyStyle);
            cells[row, 3].SetStyle(currencyStyle);
        }

        for (int row = items.Length + 3; row <= summaryRow; row++)
        {
            cells[row, 3].SetStyle(currencyStyle);
        }

        // Auto-fit columns
        worksheet.AutoFitColumns();

        // Save workbook
        workbook.Save("financial-calculator.xlsx");
        Console.WriteLine("Financial calculator created with formulas!");
    }
}
Create Sales Dashboard
using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;
using System;

class Program
{
    static void Main()
    {
        var workbook = new Workbook();
        var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];
        var cells = worksheet.Cells;

        // Set up data
        string[] months = { "Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun" };
        int[] sales = { 1200, 1500, 1100, 1800, 2000, 1700 };

        // Add data
        for (int i = 0; i < months.Length; i++)
        {
            cells[i + 2, 0].PutValue(months[i]);
            cells[i + 2, 1].PutValue(sales[i]);
        }

        // Add formulas
        int formulasStartRow = months.Length + 4;

        cells[formulasStartRow, 0].PutValue("Total Sales:");
        cells[formulasStartRow, 1].Formula = "=SUM(B2:B7)";

        formulasStartRow++;
        cells[formulasStartRow, 0].PutValue("Average Sales:");
        cells[formulasStartRow, 1].Formula = "=AVERAGE(B2:B7)";

        formulasStartRow++;
        cells[formulasStartRow, 0].PutValue("Max Sales:");
        cells[formulasStartRow, 1].Formula = "=MAX(B2:B7)";

        formulasStartRow++;
        cells[formulasStartRow, 0].PutValue("Min Sales:");
        cells[formulasStartRow, 1].Formula = "=MIN(B2:B7)";

        formulasStartRow++;
        cells[formulasStartRow, 0].PutValue("Sales Variance:");
        cells[formulasStartRow, 1].Formula = "=MAX(B2:B7)-MIN(B2:B7)";

        formulasStartRow++;
        cells[formulasStartRow, 0].PutValue("Months > 1500:");
        cells[formulasStartRow, 1].Formula = "=COUNTIF(B2:B7, \">1500\")";

        // Conditional formatting based on formulas
        formulasStartRow++;
        cells[formulasStartRow, 0].PutValue("Performance:");
        cells[formulasStartRow, 1].Formula = "=IF(B10>1500,\"Excellent\",\"Good\")";

        workbook.Save("sales-dashboard.xlsx");
        Console.WriteLine("Sales dashboard created!");
    }
}

Important Notes

  • Formula Syntax: Use standard Excel formula syntax without the '=' prefix when setting formulas.
  • Cell References: Both A1 notation (e.g., "A1") and R1C1 notation are supported.
  • Calculation: Formulas are calculated automatically when cells are modified.
  • Circular References: Circular references are detected and handled according to workbook settings.
  • Performance: For large worksheets, consider using manual calculation mode.
  • Named Ranges: Use named ranges for cleaner, more maintainable formulas.
  • Error Handling: Use IFERROR function to handle formula errors gracefully.

Related Documentation

  • Worksheet Operations - Managing worksheets and cells

  • Data Validation - Cell validation rules
  • Conditional Formatting - Advanced formatting based on formulas
